Software Requirements¶

This section specifies software-level requirements derived from the system requirements. Each requirement references its parent system requirement.

Specification: Linux Kernel SPI Driver Usage SPEC_SW_SPI_DRIVER
status: open
tags: software, spi, linux
language: C++17
layer: realtime
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_SPI
links incoming: IFACE_SPIDEV, ARCH_SW_FRAME_ASM, COMP_SPI_DRV

The realtime layer shall use the Linux spidev kernel interface to access the SPI bus (/dev/spidevX.Y).
Specification: SPI Clock Frequency SPEC_SW_SPI_CLK
status: open
tags: software, spi
language: C++17
layer: realtime
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_SPI
links incoming: ARCH_SW_FRAME_ASM, COMP_SPI_DRV

The SPI clock frequency shall be configurable and shall default to 8 MHz to satisfy the timing requirements of common LED stripe ICs (e.g. APA102).
Specification: Realtime Thread Priority SPEC_SW_RT_PRIO
status: open
tags: software, realtime, linux
language: C++17
layer: realtime
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_RT
links incoming: ARCH_SW_RT_PROC

The SPI transmission thread shall run with SCHED_FIFO scheduling policy and a priority of 80 (out of 99) to meet the 100 µs jitter requirement.
Specification: C++17 Realtime Layer SPEC_SW_CPP_RT
status: open
tags: software, realtime, cpp
language: C++17
layer: realtime
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_RT
links incoming: ARCH_SW_RT_PROC

All time-critical operations (SPI frame assembly, DMA-burst scheduling, colour-space conversion) shall be implemented in C++17.
Specification: Python High-Level API SPEC_SW_PY_API
status: open
tags: software, python, api
language: Python
layer: highlevel
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_HL
links incoming: IFACE_PY_API, ARCH_SW_PY_PROC

High-level control logic (animation sequencing, colour management, user interface) shall be implemented in Python (≥ 3.10).
Specification: IPC Between Python and C++ Layer SPEC_SW_IPC
status: open
tags: software, ipc
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_HL, REQ_SYS_RT
links incoming: IFACE_SHM_IPC, ARCH_SW_PY_PROC, ARCH_SW_IPC_PUB, ARCH_SW_SHM, COMP_IPC_PUB

The Python layer shall communicate with the C++17 realtime layer through a shared-memory ring-buffer and a POSIX named semaphore to minimise latency.
Specification: LED Frame Format SPEC_SW_FRAME
status: open
tags: software, spi, led
language: C++17
layer: realtime
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_SPI
links incoming: ARCH_SW_FRAME_ASM

The C++17 layer shall assemble SPI frames according to the APA102 protocol: start frame (32 zero bits), one 32-bit LED word per LED, and an end frame (ceiling(N/2) one-bits for N LEDs).
Specification: Graceful Shutdown Handler SPEC_SW_SHUTDOWN
status: open
tags: software, safety
language: C++17
layer: realtime
links outgoing: REQ_SYS_SAFE
links incoming: ARCH_SW_SHUTDOWN

The realtime layer shall register a handler for SIGTERM and SIGINT that sends an all-zeros SPI frame before terminating the process.
